title: Internal IT Help Desk and Ticketing System

prd.oneLiner: A help-desk system that connects employee IT and facilities requests, assignment, SLA, resolution records, knowledge articles, and operations analysis.
prd.goal: Move 70% of email and chat requests into tickets and achieve 90% or more SLA-compliant resolutions within three months.
prd.whyNow: Requests across multiple channels leave priority and ownership unclear, while the same problems are solved repeatedly.
prd.problem: Employees cannot easily track request status or expected resolution, and support teams manually chase delayed or repeated issues.
prd.solution: Provide a request portal, category and priority, queues and assignment, SLA and status notifications, resolution records, knowledge articles, and an operations dashboard.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, shared spreadsheets, and verbal requests.
prd.differentiator: Connect ticket handling with reusable knowledge and analysis of SLA and repeated issues.

targets.name: Requester
targets.needs: Quickly submit a problem and know its status and resolution path.
targets.pain: They cannot tell who owns their request or when it will be solved.

targets.name: Support agent
targets.needs: Receive prioritized work and leave a reusable resolution history.
targets.pain: Duplicate tickets and incomplete context increase handling time.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Employees cannot easily track request status or expected resolution, and support teams manually chase delayed or repeated issues.

scenarios.text: An employee searches for a VPN answer, then submits a prioritized ticket with a screenshot when it does not resolve the issue.
A support lead reviews SLA-risk tickets and reassigns ownership.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and exception handling status.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and exception handling status.

prd.northStar: Share of tickets resolved within SLA

kpis.name: SLA-compliant resolution rate
kpis.method: Tickets resolved within SLA ÷ all resolved tickets

kpis.name: Self-service resolution rate
kpis.method: Sessions that view a knowledge article without creating a ticket

inScope: Request portal and ticket creation and review
Category, priority, owner, SLA, and status management
Comments, attachments, resolution records, and notifications
Knowledge articles plus ticket and SLA operations reporting
Request portal and ticket creation validation and duplicate prevention
assumptions: Employee and support-team data are available through company SSO.
Support administrators manage request categories and SLA policies.
risks: Weak initial routing rules may assign tickets to the wrong queue.
Stale knowledge articles may reduce trust in self-service.
openQuestions: What defines an urgent request and its approval path?
How long should requester confirmation remain open before automatic closure?
constraints: Ticket status, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
